title: Non-Treating Provider — Specialty Mismatch Chart Access

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.502(a)(1) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.502(a)(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.35: Non-Treating Provider — Specialty Mismatch Chart Access.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.35' running on sourcetype epic:audit and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup provider_specialty.csv USER_ID OUTPUT specialty_group
| lookup patient_unit_map.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT unit_service_line
| eval mismatch=if(isnotnull(specialty_group) AND NOT match(unit_service_line, specialty_group),1,0)
| where mismatch=1 AND specialty_group IN ("BILLING","CODING","IT","HR")
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID, specialty_group
| where patients>=10
| sort - patients
description: Highlights when users in non-clinical specialties repeatedly open inpatient charts outside their documented specialty cohort, a common snooping pattern.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes epic and sourcetypes epic:audit, epic:audit`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Refresh patient service line nightly; (2) tune for legitimate cross-coverage; (3) route to privacy investigations with manager context. Indexes required: `index=epic`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `sourcetype=epic:audit``; Lookups: `patient_unit_map`, `patient_unit_map.csv`, `provider_specialty`, `provider_specialty.csv`.
visualization: Table (user, specialty, patients), Bar chart (violations by department).
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup provider_specialty.csv` (`USER_ID`, `specialty_group`), Clarity-derived `patient_unit_map.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `unit_service_line`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Refresh patient service line nightly; (2) tune for legitimate cross-coverage; (3) route to privacy investigations with manager context.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup provider_specialty.csv USER_ID OUTPUT specialty_group
| lookup patient_unit_map.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT unit_service_line
| eval mismatch=if(isnotnull(specialty_group) AND NOT match(unit_service_line, specialty_group),1,0)
| where mismatch=1 AND specialty_group IN ("BILLING","CODING","IT","HR")
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID, specialty_group
| where patients>=10
| sort - patients
```

Understanding this SPL

**Non-Treating Provider — Specialty Mismatch Chart Access (§164.502(a)(1))** — Highlights when users in non-clinical specialties repeatedly open inpatient charts outside their documented specialty cohort, a common snooping pattern.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup provider_specialty.csv` (`USER_ID`, `specialty_group`), Clarity-derived `patient_unit_map.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `unit_service_line`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: epic; **sourcetype**: epic:audit. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=epic, sourcetype="epic:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mismatch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where mismatch=1 AND specialty_group IN ("BILLING","CODING","IT","HR")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by USER_ID, specialty_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where patients>=10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (user, specialty, patients), Bar chart (violations by department).
grandmaExplanation: We highlights when users in non-clinical specialties repeatedly open inpatient charts outside their documented specialty cohort, a common snooping pattern. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
